title: i18n: 'Approved' label in inbox approval-notification rows
description:
## Source

Discovered during ZAI-89 visual QA — see [ZAI-115](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-115) inbox-mine-ru.png. Out-of-scope for [ZAI-89](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-89) (which targeted StatusBadge / KeyboardShortcuts / SidebarAgents / Inbox).

## Symptom

At `http://127.0.0.1:3105/SDF/inbox/mine` with `ru` locale, the `Hire Agent: CEO` notification row renders:

> Approved обновлено 6h ago

`Approved` is English while the surrounding text is Russian. This is the approval-status label rendered by the inbox approval-notification component, not the issue StatusBadge.

## Reproduction

1. `pnpm -C ui dev`
2. Open `/SDF/inbox/mine` in the browser, switch language to `ru`.
3. Look for any inbox row of type `approval` whose status is `approved` (or `rejected`/`pending`/etc.) — the status word renders untranslated.

## Suspected root cause

Likely an `approvalStatusLabel(approval.status)` call (or equivalent) returning a hardcoded English string. Search for `approvalStatusLabel` and `approval.status` rendering in the inbox components.

## Acceptance

- All approval-status labels (`approved`, `rejected`, `pending`, `expired`, `cancelled`, etc.) translated in `en`/`ru` locale files.
- Visual proof: same `/inbox/mine` row renders fully in Russian under `ru` locale.
- i18n-only diff per `scripts/check-i18n-only-diff.sh`.
- No DOM/feature changes.
